title: Gender Duel
subtitle: Every Hindi noun is masculine or feminine
tip: When you are not sure, trust the ending and tap fast. The point of the duel is to pick up the tricky words by playing, so a quick wrong guess still teaches you.

left.label: MASCULINE
bullets: Words ending in -aa are usually masculine: ladka, kamra, raasta, samosa.
Most English loanwords default to masculine: phone, scooter, computer.
Famous -ii exceptions that are still masculine: paani, aadmi, ghee.
Many consonant-ending words: ghar, naam, din, haath, kaam, ped.

right.label: FEMININE
bullets: Words ending in -ii are usually feminine: kursi, gaadi, roti, ghadi.
Sanskrit -aa words flip to feminine: bhasha, seva, katha.
Common consonant-ending feminines to memorize: kitaab, raat, baat, mez.
Vehicles and most foods/drinks lean feminine: chai, bus, gaadi.
